senses.definition: Having no records or lack of documentation.
examples: The recordless shipment arrived without any tracking information.
A recordless society would struggle to verify events.

senses.definition: Pieces of information kept for reference or evidence about events or facts.
examples: The court keeps detailed case records.
Medical records should be kept confidential.

senses.definition: To document or put information into a permanent record.
examples: The journalist records the interview for later publication.
She records the weather data every hour.

senses.definition: The size or capacity of a data record in computing or data storage.
examples: The file system reports the recordsize for each table.
Adjust the recordsize to optimize performance.

senses.definition: To replace or reseal the cork in a bottle.
examples: They recorked the wine bottle to preserve it longer.
The sommeliers recorked several bottles after tasting.

senses.definition: Having had a cork replaced in a bottle; resealed.
examples: The recorked bottle kept its aroma for days.
She handed me a recorked wine bottle.

senses.definition: The act of replacing a cork to reseal a bottle.
examples: Recorking can help preserve the wine's quality.
Efficient recorking methods are used in wine storage.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of recork; reseals bottles with cork.
examples: He recorks several bottles after tasting.
The sommelier recorks the bottle when not finished.

senses.definition: The act of crowning again; a re-enshrinement of someone or something as royal.
examples: The ceremony was a recoronation of the long-dormant crown.
The group spoke of a recoronation of the festival's founder.

senses.definition: A rarely used or hypothetical process of becoming corporeal again; not common in modern use.
examples: The manuscript mentions recorporification in mythical terms.
Scholars debated the plausibility of recorporification.

senses.definition: To cause to become corporeal again; to restore in bodily form (rare/fictional).
examples: The fantasy tale attempts to recorporify the hero after enchantment.
Some myths claim it is possible to recorporify the departed.

senses.definition: To correct again; to fix an error a second time.
examples: If the math problem is wrong, recorrect it carefully.
The editor will recorrect the manuscript.

senses.definition: The act of correcting something again; a second correction.
examples: The recorrection improved the report's accuracy.
We made a recorrection to the figure.

senses.definition: To corrupt again; to become corrupt once more after some restoration.
examples: The plan began to recorrupt after the initial reforms.
Criminal networks attempt to recorrupt oversight bodies.

senses.definition: The act or state of becoming corrupt again; a relapse into corruption.
examples: The city faced a new outbreak of recorruption.
Efforts to prevent recorruption require stronger safeguards.

senses.definition: To cost again; to assign a new or different price to something.
examples: The retailer recost the item after a price drop.
They recosted the services for the upcoming quarter.

senses.definition: To re-dress costume or attire; to attire again or differently (rare).
examples: The troupe decided to recostume the ensemble for the new act.
They recostumed the performers for the parade.

senses.definition: Having been re-dressed or re-clad in a different costume.
examples: The recostumed actors returned for the encore.
A recostumed character appeared on stage.

senses.definition: The act of changing costumes; re-dressing in different attire.
examples: The costume designer handled recostuming between scenes.
Recostuming took longer than expected.

senses.definition: To advise again; to give further counsel.
examples: The adviser will recounsel the client on the strategy.
She decided to recounsel with her legal team.

senses.definition: Having been given additional or renewed counsel.
examples: The recounseled client changed their approach.
After recounseled, the plan looked stronger.

senses.definition: The act of giving additional advice; further consultation.
examples: Recounseling helped clarify the decision.
They scheduled recounseling to review options.

senses.definition: To tell or describe what happened; to narrate.
examples: She recounted the events of the day in detail.
He recounted a funny incident from college.

senses.definition: A detailed narrative of what happened; the act of recounting.
examples: Her recount of the trip was vivid.
The prosecutor listened to the witness recounts.

senses.definition: Capable of being recounted; able to be told or described in detail.
examples: The incident is recountable in a formal report.
Only significant events are recountable.

senses.definition: A recounting or narrative of an event; a telling of what happened.
examples: The witness gave a brief recountal of the accident.
A detailed recountal followed in court.

senses.definition: Multiple recountings or narratives of events.
examples: The report includes several recountals from different witnesses.
There are inconsistent recountals of what happened.
